questions.title: Reason to Replace Bill Cassidy
questions.prompt:
Earlier in the survey, you indicated you would consider voting to replace Bill Cassidy.

Which of the following best describes your reason for feeling that way?

options.label: He voted to impeach Donald Trump
He's been in Washington too long
He's too moderate or not conservative enough
He doesn't focus enough on Louisiana issues
He's too supportive of Donald Trump
I just prefer the other candidates in the race over him
Other (please specify)

questions.title: Reason to Re-elect Bill Cassidy
questions.prompt:
Earlier in the survey, you indicated you would consider re-electing Bill Cassidy.

Which of the following best describes your reason for feeling that way?

options.label: He voted to impeach Donald Trump and I respect that decision
He represents Louisiana well in Washington and focuses on the state's interests
He is willing to make independent decisions rather than follow party pressure
His professional background in healthcare helps him understand medical and insurance issues
I like that he is a strong supporter of President Donald Trump and his policies
I just prefer him over the other candidates in the race
Other (please specify)

metadata.label: Soccer Moms
metadata.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
metadata.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

metadata.label: White Working Class
metadata.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

metadata.label: Barstool Conservatives
metadata.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
metadata.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
tooltip.body: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

metadata.label: Disaffected Young Men
metadata.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
metadata.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

tooltip.title: Disaffected Young Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

metadata.label: Hispanic Working Class
metadata.rationale: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Hispanic, No college
tooltip.body: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

metadata.label: Evangelical Republicans
metadata.rationale: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

tooltip.title: Evangelical Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Protestant
tooltip.body: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

metadata.label: MAGA Country
metadata.rationale: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

tooltip.title: MAGA Country
tooltip.subtitle: Rural, Republican, White, No college
tooltip.body: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

metadata.label: Costco Centrists
metadata.rationale: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

tooltip.title: Costco Centrists
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

metadata.label: Tradwifes
metadata.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
metadata.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

tooltip.title: Tradwifes
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

metadata.label: Young Black Men
metadata.rationale: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

tooltip.title: Young Black Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Black, Gen Z
tooltip.body: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

metadata.label: Country Club Republicans
metadata.demographic_composition: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
metadata.rationale: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

tooltip.title: Country Club Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

metadata.label: Hispanic Soccer Moms
metadata.rationale: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Hispanic, Suburban, College completed
tooltip.body: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

metadata.label: Tech Bros
metadata.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
metadata.rationale: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

tooltip.title: Tech Bros
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
tooltip.body: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

metadata.label: Kennedy Catholics
metadata.rationale: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

tooltip.title: Kennedy Catholics
tooltip.subtitle: Catholic, Suburban or Urban
tooltip.body: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

metadata.label: Red State Women
metadata.demographic_composition: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
metadata.rationale: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

tooltip.title: Red State Women
tooltip.subtitle: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

metadata.label: Blue-Collar Men
metadata.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
metadata.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

tooltip.title: Blue-Collar Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

metadata.label: GenX Forgotten Middle
metadata.rationale: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

tooltip.title: GenX Forgotten Middle
tooltip.subtitle: GenX, Other party affiliation, Suburban
tooltip.body: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

metadata.label: Secular Suburbanites
metadata.demographic_composition: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
metadata.rationale: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

tooltip.title: Secular Suburbanites
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

metadata.label: Black Rural Working Class
metadata.rationale: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

tooltip.title: Black Rural Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Black - rural - no college

in_washington_too_long.label: Soccer Moms
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms

tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. in_washington_too_long.label: White Working Class
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

in_washington_too_long.label: Barstool Conservatives
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
in_washington_too_long.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.label: White Working Class
does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.label: Barstool Conservatives
does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.label: Tradwifes
does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. tooltip.body: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Costco Centrists
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

tooltip.title: Costco Centrists
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Tradwifes
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Country Club Republicans

prefer_other_candidates.label: Blue-Collar Men
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

other_specify.label: Soccer Moms
other_specify.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
other_specify.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms

other_specify.label: White Working Class
other_specify.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

other_specify.label: Barstool Conservatives
other_specify.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
other_specify.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

in_washington_too_long.label: Soccer Moms
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

in_washington_too_long.label: White Working Class

in_washington_too_long.label: Barstool Conservatives
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
in_washington_too_long.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

other_specify.label: Soccer Moms

other_specify.label: White Working Class

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

other_specify.label: Barstool Conservatives
other_specify.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
other_specify.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

in_washington_too_long.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

tooltip.title: Disaffected Young Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

in_washington_too_long.label: Hispanic Working Class
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Hispanic, No college
tooltip.body: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

in_washington_too_long.label: Evangelical Republicans
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

tooltip.title: Evangelical Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Protestant
tooltip.body: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

in_washington_too_long.label: MAGA Country
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

tooltip.title: MAGA Country
tooltip.subtitle: Rural, Republican, White, No college
tooltip.body: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

in_washington_too_long.label: Costco Centrists
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

in_washington_too_long.label: Tradwifes
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
in_washington_too_long.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

in_washington_too_long.label: Red State Women
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
in_washington_too_long.rationale: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

in_washington_too_long.label: Blue-Collar Men
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: Soccer Moms
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: White Working Class
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: Barstool Conservatives
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Soccer Moms
too_supportive_of_trump.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. too_supportive_of_trump.label: White Working Class
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Barstool Conservatives
too_supportive_of_trump.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

other_specify.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

other_specify.label: White Working Class
other_specify.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

other_specify.label: Barstool Conservatives
other_specify.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
other_specify.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

other_specify.label: Tradwifes
other_specify.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
other_specify.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. in_washington_too_long.rationale: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

in_washington_too_long.label: Red State Women

in_washington_too_long.label: Blue-Collar Men
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: Soccer Moms
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: White Working Class
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: Barstool Conservatives
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

impeach_trump_vote.label: Barstool Conservatives
impeach_trump_vote.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
impeach_trump_vote.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

in_washington_too_long.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

tooltip.title: Blue-Collar Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

in_washington_too_long.label: GenX Forgotten Middle
in_washington_too_long.rationale: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

tooltip.title: GenX Forgotten Middle
tooltip.subtitle: GenX, Other party affiliation, Suburban
tooltip.body: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

in_washington_too_long.label: Secular Suburbanites
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

tooltip.title: Secular Suburbanites
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree

in_washington_too_long.label: Black Rural Working Class
in_washington_too_long.rationale: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

tooltip.title: Black Rural Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Black - rural - no college
tooltip.body: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: Soccer Moms
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: White Working Class
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: Barstool Conservatives
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: Disaffected Young Men
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

tooltip.title: Evangelical Republicans

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: MAGA Country

tooltip.title: MAGA Country

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.label: Tradwifes
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

impeach_trump_vote.label: Soccer Moms
impeach_trump_vote.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
impeach_trump_vote.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

impeach_trump_vote.label: White Working Class
impeach_trump_vote.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

impeach_trump_vote.label: Barstool Conservatives
impeach_trump_vote.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
impeach_trump_vote.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives

impeach_trump_vote.label: Disaffected Young Men
impeach_trump_vote.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation

too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
too_moderate_not_conservative_enough.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

tooltip.title: Tradwifes
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Young Black Men
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

tooltip.title: Young Black Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Black, Gen Z
tooltip.body: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Country Club Republicans
too_supportive_of_trump.demographic_composition: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

tooltip.title: Country Club Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Hispanic Soccer Moms
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Hispanic, Suburban, College completed
tooltip.body: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Tech Bros
too_supportive_of_trump.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

tooltip.title: Tech Bros
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
tooltip.body: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Kennedy Catholics
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

tooltip.title: Kennedy Catholics
tooltip.subtitle: Catholic, Suburban or Urban
tooltip.body: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Red State Women
too_supportive_of_trump.demographic_composition: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

tooltip.title: Red State Women
tooltip.subtitle: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Blue-Collar Men
too_supportive_of_trump.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

tooltip.title: Blue-Collar Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural

too_supportive_of_trump.label: GenX Forgotten Middle
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

tooltip.title: GenX Forgotten Middle
tooltip.subtitle: GenX, Other party affiliation, Suburban
tooltip.body: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Secular Suburbanites
too_supportive_of_trump.demographic_composition: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

tooltip.title: Secular Suburbanites
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

too_supportive_of_trump.label: Black Rural Working Class
too_supportive_of_trump.rationale: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

tooltip.title: Black Rural Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Black - rural - no college
tooltip.body: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Soccer Moms
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

prefer_other_candidates.label: White Working Class
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Barstool Conservatives
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives

prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

impeach_trump_vote.label: Soccer Moms

impeach_trump_vote.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

impeach_trump_vote.label: White Working Class

impeach_trump_vote.label: Barstool Conservatives
impeach_trump_vote.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
impeach_trump_vote.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.label: Barstool Conservatives

Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

prefer_other_candidates.label: White Working Class

prefer_other_candidates.label: Barstool Conservatives
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.label: Barstool Conservatives
does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
does_not_focus_on_louisiana_issues.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

prefer_other_candidates.label: White Working Class
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Barstool Conservatives
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
tooltip.body: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. prefer_other_candidates.label: Disaffected Young Men
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Evangelical Republicans

tooltip.title: Evangelical Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Protestant
tooltip.body: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

prefer_other_candidates.label: MAGA Country
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

tooltip.title: MAGA Country
tooltip.subtitle: Rural, Republican, White, No college
tooltip.body: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Costco Centrists

tooltip.title: Costco Centrists

prefer_other_candidates.label: Tradwifes
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

tooltip.title: Country Club Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

impeach_trump_vote.label: Hispanic Soccer Moms
impeach_trump_vote.rationale: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Hispanic, Suburban, College completed
tooltip.body: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

impeach_trump_vote.label: Kennedy Catholics
impeach_trump_vote.rationale: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

tooltip.title: Kennedy Catholics
tooltip.subtitle: Catholic, Suburban or Urban
tooltip.body: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

impeach_trump_vote.label: Red State Women
impeach_trump_vote.demographic_composition: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
impeach_trump_vote.rationale: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

tooltip.title: Red State Women
tooltip.subtitle: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

impeach_trump_vote.label: Blue-Collar Men
impeach_trump_vote.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
impeach_trump_vote.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

tooltip.title: Blue-Collar Men

impeach_trump_vote.label: GenX Forgotten Middle

tooltip.subtitle: GenX, Other party affiliation, Suburban

impeach_trump_vote.label: Secular Suburbanites

tooltip.subtitle: Black - rural - no college
tooltip.body: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

in_washington_too_long.label: Soccer Moms
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

in_washington_too_long.label: White Working Class
in_washington_too_long.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

in_washington_too_long.label: Barstool Conservatives
in_washington_too_long.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
in_washington_too_long.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives

in_washington_too_long.label: Disaffected Young Men

tooltip.title: MAGA Country
tooltip.subtitle: Rural, Republican, White, No college
tooltip.body: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Costco Centrists
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

tooltip.title: Costco Centrists
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Tradwifes
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

tooltip.title: Tradwifes
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural

prefer_other_candidates.label: Young Black Men
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

tooltip.title: Young Black Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Black, Gen Z
tooltip.body: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Country Club Republicans
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

tooltip.title: Country Club Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Hispanic Soccer Moms
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Hispanic, Suburban, College completed
tooltip.body: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Tech Bros
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

tooltip.title: Tech Bros
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
tooltip.body: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Kennedy Catholics
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

tooltip.title: Kennedy Catholics
tooltip.subtitle: Catholic, Suburban or Urban
tooltip.body: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Red State Women
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

tooltip.title: Red State Women
tooltip.subtitle: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Blue-Collar Men
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

tooltip.title: Blue-Collar Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

prefer_other_candidates.label: GenX Forgotten Middle
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

tooltip.title: GenX Forgotten Middle
tooltip.subtitle: GenX, Other party affiliation, Suburban
tooltip.body: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Secular Suburbanites
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

tooltip.title: Secular Suburbanites
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Black Rural Working Class
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

tooltip.title: Black Rural Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Black - rural - no college
tooltip.body: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

other_specify.label: Soccer Moms
other_specify.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
other_specify.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

other_specify.label: White Working Class
other_specify.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

other_specify.label: Barstool Conservatives
other_specify.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
other_specify.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives

in_washington_too_long.label: Barstool Conservatives

prefer_other_candidates.label: Kennedy Catholics

tooltip.body: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Red State Women
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

prefer_other_candidates.label: Blue-Collar Men
prefer_other_candidates.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
prefer_other_candidates.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.
